To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and then form the polynomial in $ \lambda $.

---

### Step 1: Write the matrix $ A - \lambda I $

Given matrix:
$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Then,
$$
A - \lambda I = 
\begin{pmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $

We compute:
$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

---

Computing this determinant manually is tedious and error-prone. Instead, we can use a **symbolic computation** approach or use a **determinant calculator**.
